meaning: 9trAnsfE`meIFalism/9trAnsfE`malist/9trAnsfE`mally/9trAnsfE`meist/9trAnsfE` men/n.变化,转化,改EnEl/adj.[语]转籩IFEnElIzEm/n.[觘IFEnElIst/n.[语IFEnElI/adv.[语]IFEnIst/n.=trans适,改革,转换
examples: The transformation of American sea power began in 1940.
Norah made plans for the transformation of an attic room into a study.
The way in which we work has undergone a complete transformation in the past decade.
But a modest transformation of the way you look at your enterprise or organization offers extraordinary advantages.
Doubtless, the next president will implement policies and take credit for helping this inevitable transformation.
